title: Cache D1 /components/list responses in KV (HTML + JSON, SWR)
description: Caches D1 componentslist responses in KV for both HTML and JSON formats to reduce repeated query work and improve response times.
body:
### Motivation
- Reduce repeated D1 query work for `/components/list` by caching both HTML and JSON representations in KV so subsequent reloads are fast. 
- Avoid collisions between HTML and JSON cache entries for the same pathname+query by differentiating representations.

### Description
- Pass the shared `CacheService` and `ExecutionContext` into `tryD1Route` and use a new `handleD1ComponentsListWithCache` to serve and populate KV entries with `HIT`/`MISS`/`STALE` semantics. 
- Introduce representation-aware cache keys by appending a `__format` query param (`json` or `html`) via `getD1RepresentationCacheUrl` so HTML and JSON do not collide. 
- Implement stale-while-revalidate behavior for D1 components responses by returning stale entries immediately and scheduling a background refresh with `ctx.waitUntil` (`refreshStaleD1ComponentsEntry`). 
- Add two integration tests that verify serving cached D1 components HTML and JSON from KV (`cf-proxy/test/worker.test.ts`).

### Testing
- Ran `bun test cf-proxy/test/worker.test.ts` which initially errored due to a missing `kysely-d1` package in the test environment, then installed dependencies with `bun install` and re-ran the tests. 
- After installing deps, `bun test cf-proxy/test/worker.test.ts` passed (all new and existing integration tests passed). 
- Type-checked the project with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which succeeded. 
- Ran formatter with `bun run format` which completed successfully.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_69cc18ec85fc832eb2d9025de9224c84)

title: This PR updates the CLI docs to cover several `tsci` options that were missing or incomplete across the command reference pages.
description: This PR updates the CLI documentation to include missing tsci options and corrects existing descriptions, enhancing the clarity and completeness of the command reference pages.
body:
## Summary

This PR updates the CLI docs to cover several `tsci` options that were missing or incomplete across the command reference pages.

## What changed

- added missing `tsci build` flags, including:
  - `--routing-disabled`
  - `--pcb-png`
  - `--3d-png` and clarified `--3d` as its alias
  - `--show-courtyards`
  - `--kicad-project-zip`
  - `--inject-props`
  - `--inject-props-file`
- fixed and normalized some existing `tsci build` option descriptions
- documented `--show-courtyards` for `tsci export`
- added an example for exporting PCB SVGs with courtyard outlines
- documented additional `tsci snapshot` flags, including:
  - `--show-courtyards`
  - `--camera-preset`
  - `--ci`
  - `--test`
  - `--concurrency`

## Why

The command reference was missing several supported flags, which made the docs incomplete and harder to trust when using the CLI. This PR brings the docs closer to the actual interface and makes less-common but useful options easier to discover.

## Scope

Documentation only. No runtime or algorithmic changes.

title: chore: bump version to v0.0.383

title: Fix stale fixed-via integration by removing manual convex graph bootstrapping and adopting `FixedViaHypergraphSolver` auto-convex API
description: Removes manual convex graph bootstrapping in favor of the FixedViaHypergraphSolver auto-convex API for improved integration and reduced maintenance risk.
body:
This PR resolves an integration mismatch where `FixedTopologyHighDensityIntraNodeSolver` was still manually building convex via graphs while `fixed-via-hypergraph-solver` now supports one-call auto-convex setup.

### What changed
- Removed direct `createConvexViaGraphFromXYConnections` usage in intra-node routing.
- Switched initialization to `new FixedViaHypergraphSolver({ inputConnections })`.
- Removed obsolete `tiledViasByNet` state that was only needed for manual graph assembly.
- Updated `@tscircuit/fixed-via-hypergraph-solver` pin to latest merged commit:  
  `bed37a6201b5dd07c9cc68b828917ecc20d6d049`.

### Impact
- Aligns autorouter with the new upstream API.
- Reduces integration glue and duplicate graph-construction logic.
- Lowers maintenance risk by relying on the solver’s canonical internal convex/tile setup.

title: Fix obstacle pcb_port attribution in circuit-json conversion
description: Fixes incorrect attribution of pcb_port in circuit-json conversion by selecting the nearest pcb_port_id for obstacles instead of the first connected port, ensuring accurate DRC labels and alignment with obstacle geometry.
body:
Choose the nearest pcb_port_id for converted obstacles instead of taking the first pcb_port_* entry from connectedTo. 
This keeps pad and plated-hole attribution aligned with obstacle geometry and avoids misleading DRC labels when multiple ports are listed on the same obstacle metadata.

title: Use emoji status indicators in benchmark comment script
description: Changes the benchmark GitHub comment script to use emoji indicators for test status instead of verbose text labels.
body:
### Motivation
- Make the per-test status and relaxed DRC fields in the benchmark GitHub comment more compact and visually clear by replacing verbose labels with emoji markers.

### Description
- Updated the inline Node.js script in `.github/workflows/benchmark.yml` to change `getStatus` and `getDrc` to return `✅`/`❌` emoji instead of the previous text values (e.g. `solved`/`failed`/`timed out` and `pass`/`fail`/`n/a`).

### Testing
- Performed YAML validation and a Node syntax/lint check against the modified workflow file and they completed successfully.

title: chore: bump version to v0.0.377
description: Automated package update
body: Automated package update

title: Fix zoom-induced dashed-trace rendering bug in `HighDensitySolver` by using scale-stable dash units
description: Fixes visualization issue where non-primary-layer traces appeared as near-continuous lines when zooming in by replacing fixed string dash pattern with numeric dash units and reducing non-primary-layer transparency for improved trace visibility.
body:
This PR fixes a visualization issue where non-primary-layer traces appeared as near-continuous lines when zooming in.

- Replaces fixed string dash pattern (`"10, 5"`) with numeric dash units (`[0.1, 0.3]`) for `segment.z !== 0`, so dash behavior remains consistent under zoom/scaling.
- Reduces non-primary-layer transparency (`safeTransparentize(..., 0.75 -> 0.5)`) to improve trace visibility while preserving top-vs-bottom layer distinction.
- Scope is visualization-only in `HighDensitySolver`; routing logic and geometry are unchanged.


### After

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e147336a-1b3e-4067-a03a-363400ff04c6


### Before 
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/349683bd-7e49-46f9-999f-acdc07f8108b

title: Reduce repair solver conflict geometry by collapsing collinear segment chains
description: Reduces the geometry complexity for conflict detection in the high-density repair solver by collapsing collinear segment chains into single segments, improving performance without altering route points or solver output.
body:
This speeds up the high-density repair solver by simplifying only the geometry used for conflict detection. Long same-layer collinear
  point chains are collapsed into single geometry segments in getRouteGeometry, while the actual route points and solver output remain
  unchanged.

  Benchmark on scripts/benchmark-first-1000.ts --limit 1000:

  - Before: 4696.31ms
  - After: 4297.13ms
  - Improvement: about 8.5%

title: speedup
description: Reduces benchmark time from 5078ms to 4581ms by optimizing conflict detection and move evaluation in the routing algorithm.
body:
- conflict detection now uses cached route bounds and cheap AABB pruning
  - single-route move evaluation skips redundant final conflict scans
  - benchmark moved from 5078ms to 4581ms on the first 1000 samples

title: Fix endpoint drift away from boundary edges in repair repros
description: Preserves route endpoints on any node boundary side they originally touch exactly, so repair moves no longer pull terminals off the graph edge in the cmn_72 and cmn_79 repros.
body: Preserves route endpoints on any node boundary side they originally touch exactly, so repair moves no longer pull terminals off the graph edge in the cmn_72 and cmn_79 repros.

title: Reduce solver overhead by skipping progress frame capture by default
description: Reduces solver overhead by skipping progress frame capture during normal solves and benchmarks, while allowing for explicit captureProgressFrames opt-in for full debugger visualization.
body:
This makes the solver faster by avoiding expensive intermediate frame cloning/building during normal solves and
  benchmarks, while preserving full debugger visualization behind an explicit captureProgressFrames opt-in. It also trims
  some queue overhead in movable/pushable index discovery.

  Benchmark on benchmark:first-1000 improved from 6220.06ms total / 6.22ms average to 5482.98ms total / 5.48ms average
